title: TacticalJudge eval scores — overall + per-dimension + per-archetype
description: Phase 12 LLM-as-Judge offline eval surface. UNLIKE the other dashboards, this consumes JSON-lines artifacts written by `python -m eldritch_dm.eval.cli` (see src/eldritch_dm/eval/runner.py + aggregator.py), NOT live Phoenix spans — eval is an offline regression-test loop, not an online signal. The phoenix_project_name field is kept for seed-script consistency. KPIs: overall_score trend per release, per-dimension floor (tactics / role-play / mechanics), per-archetype scoreboard.
query_recipe:
**OFFLINE DASHBOARD — Phoenix UI is not the primary consumer.** Source: per-run JSON-lines artifacts written by `python -m eldritch_dm.eval.cli` (default output dir: `eval-outputs/<run_id>.jsonl`). Each line is an EvalAggregate (see src/eldritch_dm/eval/aggregator.py: fields `overall_score`, `per_dimension_mean`, `per_archetype_mean`, `n_scenarios`, plus per-scenario `verdict.overall_score` and `verdict.per_dimension`).

Render via a notebook or BI tool (Phoenix UI cannot ingest these natively):
  1. Overall score over time (line): plot `EvalAggregate.overall_score` against run timestamp across the last 30d of runs. Healthy: monotonic-ish improvement or stable >7.5 floor.
  2. Per-dimension breakdown (radar or bar): `per_dimension_mean` for the most-recent run — tactics, role-play, mechanics, judgement, narration etc. Alert when any dimension drops below CRITICAL_DIM_MIN (currently 5.0 — see aggregator.py::CRITICAL_DIM_MIN).
  3. Per-archetype scoreboard (table): `per_archetype_mean` sorted descending. Identifies which monster archetypes the SmartMonsterDriver handles well vs. badly. Target: no archetype <6.0 mean over 3-run rolling window.
  4. Per-scenario drill-down: for any run, expand `results[]` to inspect individual verdicts and judge rationales — useful when overall_score regresses.

To also see the eval runs as span traces (latency, tokens, cost) cross-reference Phoenix project 'eldritch-eval-spans' (NOT this project) — `eldritch.eval` spans land in the span buffer via the standard observability path and feed the eldritch-budget dashboard's spend trend.
